steps.title: Apply the volume comparison
steps.content: For a shared favorite recording, musicians tended to prefer a higher optimal volume than nonmusicians. If the musician's optimal volume for "Sad but True" is 85.5 decibels, the nonmusician's optimal volume would be expected to be lower.

steps.title: Remove unsupported claims
steps.content: Choice A compares music with nature sounds, but the finding compares listeners' optimal volumes for the same recording. Choice B invents a preference for other music. Choice C overstates the finding by saying 85.5 decibels would be too low for any recording. The remaining choice says 85.5 decibels would be less enjoyable than a lower volume for this song. The answer is D.
